name: enterpriseai.tools tools dataset
description: Source-backed governance posture for every tracked enterprise AI tool. Each record carries data residency, deployment model, audit logging, SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / ISO 42001, EU AI Act role, and license risk with a primary source URL on every asserted claim.
datasetLicenseNote: Publication licence for this dataset file. Each record's own licence is in its `license` field.

tools.name: Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
tools.description: Fully managed agent platform with deep Microsoft 365 integration and broad model access.
tools.pricing: Free platform access; pay for tokens and tools.
tools.logoNotes: Official Microsoft Foundry product image/logo asset reused for Foundry Agent Service. Microsoft has a dedicated Agent Service product page, but the public visual identity still appears to reuse the broader Foundry branding rather than expose a distinct standalone mark.
tools.practitionerNote: Best fit when agent delivery needs to stay close to Azure, M365, and enterprise identity controls rather than a standalone framework stack.

dataResidency.detail: EU Data Zone pins prompts/responses to EU; Global Standard may cross region.

deployment.detail: Managed SaaS on Azure; Foundry Local enables on-device/on-prem.

auditLogging.detail: Audit/RequestResponse/Trace diagnostic logs to Azure Monitor / Log Analytics.

soc2.detail: Azure SOC 2 Type II (semi-annual) covers AI Foundry.

iso27001.detail: Covered by Microsoft Azure ISO/IEC 27001 certification.

iso42001.detail: Microsoft Foundry is listed in scope for ISO/IEC 42001:2023.

euAiAct.detail: Agent hosting surface; GPAI obligations attach to the underlying model providers and the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary Azure SaaS; moderate ecosystem lock-in.

tools.name: Amazon Bedrock Agents
tools.description: Modular AWS agent platform combining guided Bedrock Agents with framework-agnostic AgentCore deployment.
tools.pricing: Pay-per-use for Bedrock and AgentCore features.
tools.logoNotes: Official AWS architecture icon for Amazon Bedrock (`Arch_Amazon-Bedrock_64.svg` from the AWS Architecture Icons pack), intentionally reused for Bedrock Agents because AWS currently presents Agents as a Bedrock capability rather than with a separate public icon.
tools.practitionerNote: Strong choice for AWS-centric teams that want managed agent primitives and infrastructure alignment without standardising on one open source framework.

dataResidency.detail: Region-selected; cross-region inference stays within the chosen geography (US/EU/JP/AU).

deployment.detail: Fully managed AWS SaaS; AgentCore runtime is AWS-operated.

auditLogging.detail: CloudTrail logs all API activity; CloudWatch metrics; optional S3/CloudWatch storage.

soc2.detail: Amazon Bedrock is in scope for AWS SOC 1/2/3 (Type II via AWS Artifact).

iso27001.detail: Amazon Bedrock is ISO/IEC 27001 certified.

iso42001.detail: Amazon Bedrock is one of four AWS services in accredited ISO/IEC 42001:2023 scope.

euAiAct.detail: Agent orchestration platform; GPAI obligations attach to model providers and the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary AWS SaaS; lock-in to AWS model catalog and AgentCore runtime.

tools.name: Google Agent Builder + ADK
tools.description: Open platform pairing the Agent Development Kit with managed agent services on Vertex AI.
tools.logoNotes: Official Google Cloud Vertex AI icon from the Google Cloud icon library, intentionally reused for Agent Builder + ADK because Google positions the managed agent surface inside Vertex AI / Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform rather than with a separate public icon entry.
tools.practitionerNote: Useful when Vertex AI, search, and Google-first app surfaces matter, but maturity and enterprise standardisation still need close validation.

dataResidency.detail: US/EU multi-region controls (CMEK, VPC-SC); not all features single-region. ADK self-hosted is customer-controlled.

deployment.detail: Managed Agent Builder on Vertex AI (SaaS); ADK is an Apache-2.0 SDK deployable anywhere.

auditLogging.detail: Cloud Audit Logs record admin/data operations; Agent Engine supports Cloud Logging/Trace.

soc2.detail: Google Cloud / Vertex AI holds SOC 1/2/3.

iso42001.detail: Google Cloud holds ISO/IEC 42001 broadly; Agent Builder not individually enumerated in the public scope.

euAiAct.detail: Agent orchestration platform/SDK; GPAI obligations attach to Google as model provider and to deployers.

licenseRisk.detail: Managed layer proprietary (Vertex AI); ADK is Apache-2.0. Blended medium.

tools.name: Databricks Mosaic AI Agent Framework
tools.description: Databricks-native agent stack combining managed tools, MCP connectivity, retrieval, and MLflow-backed evaluation.
strengths: Managed MCP servers for Databricks data
Vector Search and Unity Catalog integration
tools.pricing: Databricks platform consumption; feature-specific pricing depends on serving, storage, and compute.
tools.logoNotes: Official Databricks wordmark reused across Databricks product entries because the public Databricks brand portal exposes the vendor lockup, while these specific product pages do not expose distinct standalone product marks.
tools.practitionerNote: Best fit when agent delivery needs to stay close to Databricks-native data, governed tools, and evaluation workflows rather than a general-purpose app stack.

dataResidency.detail: Databricks Geos enforce per-region residency; data not stored outside the workspace Geo.

deployment.detail: Managed Databricks platform on AWS/Azure/GCP; no standalone self-hosted offering.

auditLogging.detail: Audit log system tables capture hundreds of workspace/account action types.

soc2.detail: Databricks SOC 2 Type II across AWS/Azure/GCP, refreshed three times yearly.

iso27001.detail: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified across all three clouds.

iso42001.detail: No public ISO 42001 certification listed on the Databricks trust pages as of 2026-05.

euAiAct.detail: Developer toolset on a data platform; deployer carries obligations for systems built with it.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary SaaS; lock-in to Unity Catalog / Vector Search / MLflow stack.

tools.name: CrewAI
tools.description: Open source multi-agent framework focused on autonomous teams and task coordination.
tools.logoNotes: Official CrewAI primary logo asset from the CrewAI brand resource center (`Logo.svg`).
tools.practitionerNote: Good for fast multi-agent prototypes, but larger regulated deployments usually need extra guardrails around observability, change control, and execution boundaries.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted library; data residency is fully operator-controlled.

deployment.detail: pip-installed OSS framework; runs on operator infrastructure (managed cloud sold separately).

auditLogging.detail: No built-in audit subsystem; operators add logging via standard tooling/integrations.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: AutoGen
tools.description: Multi-agent framework from Microsoft now in maintenance mode after Agent Framework consolidation.
tools.statusNote: Maintenance mode. Microsoft merged AutoGen with Semantic Kernel into the Microsoft Agent Framework.
tools.logoNotes: Official AutoGen logo asset from the owner-controlled microsoft/autogen repo (`python/docs/src/_static/images/logo/logo.svg`).
tools.practitionerNote: Flexible for research-heavy agent patterns, though teams normally need to add stronger production discipline around orchestration and runtime governance.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted library; data residency is operator-controlled.

deployment.detail: pip-installed OSS framework; no Microsoft-managed hosting (maintenance mode).

auditLogging.detail: No built-in audit subsystem; operator implements logging.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: Code MIT (docs CC BY 4.0) — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: LlamaIndex
tools.description: Agent and retrieval framework with strong indexing, data connectors, and orchestration support.
tools.logoNotes: Official LlamaIndex brand asset from the LlamaIndex brand guidelines page (`llamaindex-wordmark-black.svg`).
tools.practitionerNote: Often a pragmatic fit when retrieval and data connectors matter as much as agent logic, especially for knowledge-heavy enterprise workflows.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted library; operator controls data placement.

deployment.detail: pip-installed OSS framework; no managed hosting for the library.

auditLogging.detail: No native audit log; tracing/callback integrations available, operator-configured.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Semantic Kernel
tools.description: Microsoft orchestration SDK evolving into the unified Microsoft Agent Framework direction.
tools.statusNote: Evolving into the Microsoft Agent Framework.
tools.logoNotes: Official Semantic Kernel logo asset from the owner-controlled microsoft/semantic-kernel repo (`docs/images/sk_logo.png`).
tools.practitionerNote: Natural fit for Microsoft-leaning engineering teams that want code-first orchestration with .NET and enterprise integration paths.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted SDK; data residency is operator-determined.

deployment.detail: Library/SDK via package manager; no managed hosting.

auditLogging.detail: OpenTelemetry integration documented but operator-configured; no built-in audit log.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
tools.description: Microsoft's new multi-language agent framework for building, orchestrating, and deploying agent workflows.
tools.statusNote: Microsoft positions Agent Framework as the migration target for Semantic Kernel and AutoGen users.
tools.logoNotes: Official Microsoft Agent Framework logo asset from the owner-controlled microsoft/agent-framework repo (`python/packages/devui/frontend/public/agentframework.svg`), committed byte-for-byte.
tools.practitionerNote: Worth watching for teams standardising on Microsoft agent patterns, but adoption should still be treated as early and architecture-led.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted framework; data residency is operator-determined.

deployment.detail: Library via package manager (Python/NuGet); no managed hosting.

auditLogging.detail: DevUI tracing for local dev; production logging operator-configured.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: LangGraph
tools.description: Stateful agent orchestration framework with graph-based execution and durable workflows.
tools.logoNotes: Official LangGraph logo asset from the owner-controlled langchain-ai/langgraph repo (`.github/images/logo-light.svg`).
tools.practitionerNote: Strong option when teams want explicit stateful control over agent flows instead of opaque black-box autonomy.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted library; operator controls placement (LangSmith cloud has separate terms).

deployment.detail: OSS library (self-hosted); LangGraph Cloud via LangSmith is an optional SaaS.

auditLogging.detail: No built-in audit log; tracing via LangSmith (separate service); operator implements logging.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Mastra
tools.description: Agent framework focused on composable workflows, tooling, and modern developer ergonomics.
tools.logoNotes: Official Mastra homepage/brand asset (`/brand/mastra-logo-wordmark.png`) from the owner-controlled site; the public rebrand post confirms this is the current logo system.
tools.licenseWarning: Mastra uses a dual-license model: most of the repo is Apache 2.0, while code under ee/ directories uses the Mastra Enterprise License.
tools.practitionerNote: Promising TypeScript-first framework for product teams that want modern developer ergonomics, but enterprise proof points are still earlier than older stacks.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted framework; operator controls placement.

deployment.detail: npm-installed framework; no managed hosting for the OSS core.

auditLogging.detail: No built-in enterprise audit log; observability integrations, operator-configured.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: Apache-2.0 core; ee/ paths under a proprietary Mastra Enterprise License (source-available).

tools.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
tools.description: Production successor to Swarm for agentic OpenAI workflows with broader model support.
tools.statusNote: OpenAI Swarm was replaced by the Agents SDK.
tools.logoNotes: OpenAI does not expose a standalone public Agents SDK logo on the official guide page; this uses the owner-controlled OpenAI developer favicon (`/favicon.svg`) as a compact service icon for the SDK surface rather than claiming a separate canonical vendor wordmark.
tools.practitionerNote: Fast path for OpenAI-centric agent builds, though portability and provider concentration should be weighed before making it a platform default.

dataResidency.detail: Library is self-hosted, but built-in tracing sends run data to OpenAI's platform unless disabled.

deployment.detail: pip-installed SDK (self-hosted); API calls go to OpenAI's hosted endpoints.

auditLogging.detail: Hosted tracing logs runs to OpenAI's platform; self-hosted logging is operator-implemented.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Atomic Agents
tools.description: Minimalist agent framework emphasizing composability and predictable building blocks.
tools.logoNotes: Official Atomic Agents logo asset from the owner-controlled BrainBlend-AI/atomic-agents repo (`.assets/logo.png`), committed here as an optimized downscaled copy for badge use.
tools.practitionerNote: Best suited to teams that want minimal abstractions and tight control, not large batteries-included enterprise workflow tooling.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted library; operator controls placement.

deployment.detail: pip-installed OSS framework; no managed hosting.

auditLogging.detail: Minimalist design; observability is intentionally operator-configured.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: BeeAI Framework
tools.description: Open agent framework formerly known as Bee Agent Framework, now under Linux Foundation governance.
tools.statusNote: Renamed from Bee Agent Framework; governance moved from IBM to Linux Foundation.
tools.logoNotes: Official BeeAI Framework docs-site logo asset (`beeai-framework-light.svg`) served from the owner-controlled Mintlify CDN.
tools.practitionerNote: Interesting IBM-backed open framework for governed agent patterns, but most teams should still validate ecosystem depth before broad standardisation.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted library; operator controls placement.

deployment.detail: npm/pip-installed framework under Linux Foundation governance; no managed hosting.

auditLogging.detail: No built-in audit log; operator-configured logging.

soc2.detail: Self-hosted library; SOC 2 applies to the operator's infrastructure, not the package.

iso27001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 27001 applies to the operator's ISMS, not the package.

iso42001.detail: Self-hosted library; ISO 42001 applies to the deploying organisation.

euAiAct.detail: Developer component, not an AI system placed on the market; obligations rest with the deployer.

licenseRisk.detail: Apache-2.0 — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Azure Logic Apps
tools.description: iPaaS workflow automation service with AI-enhanced orchestration and broad connector coverage.
tools.logoNotes: Official Microsoft Azure architecture/service icon from Azure_Public_Service_Icons_V23.
tools.practitionerNote: Best when the core problem is governed enterprise workflow automation with Azure connectors, not frontier agent behaviour or open-ended autonomy.

dataResidency.detail: Standard/ASE v3 keep data in-region; Consumption uses paired-region replication.

deployment.detail: Consumption (multi-tenant), Standard single-tenant, ASE v3, and Hybrid (on-prem via Container Apps).

auditLogging.detail: Run/trigger history and diagnostics to Azure Monitor / Log Analytics.

soc2.detail: In-scope Azure service under SOC 2 Type 2.

iso27001.detail: In-scope Azure service under ISO/IEC 27001:2022.

iso42001.detail: Microsoft's ISO 42001 scope names Copilot products and Foundry; Logic Apps is not individually listed.

euAiAct.detail: General-purpose workflow automation, not an AI system; obligations depend on integrated components.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary Microsoft service; no source access.

tools.name: AWS Step Functions
tools.description: Serverless state machine orchestration service for AWS-native workflows and automation.
tools.logoNotes: Official AWS architecture icon from Icon-package_01302026.
tools.practitionerNote: Strong fit for AWS-native production workflows that need explicit state, retries, and operational control rather than a higher-level AI builder UX.

dataResidency.detail: Regional service; executions and state remain in the selected AWS region.

deployment.detail: Fully managed serverless SaaS on AWS; no self-hosted option.

auditLogging.detail: Native execution history; Express history to CloudWatch; CloudTrail captures API calls.

soc2.detail: Explicitly in scope for AWS SOC 1/2/3.

iso27001.detail: Named in the AWS ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certificate.

iso42001.detail: AWS ISO 42001 scope covers Bedrock/Q Business/Textract/Transcribe only; Step Functions is not AI and not in scope.

euAiAct.detail: Serverless state-machine orchestration, not an AI system.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary AWS service; no source access.

tools.name: Google Cloud Workflows
tools.description: Serverless workflow orchestration for Google Cloud services and HTTP APIs.
tools.pricing: $0.01 per 1K internal steps; $0.025 per 1K external steps.
tools.logoNotes: Official Google Cloud workflow icon from the Google Cloud icon library.
tools.practitionerNote: Pragmatic choice for GCP-centric teams orchestrating APIs and services, but usually not the first pick for richer agent-builder experiences.

dataResidency.detail: Regional resource; resource-location policies enforce residency (outbound calls may differ).

deployment.detail: Fully managed serverless SaaS on Google Cloud; no self-hosted option.

auditLogging.detail: Cloud Audit Logs capture admin-activity and data-access events via workflows.googleapis.com.

soc2.detail: Runs on Google Cloud, which holds SOC 2 Type II.

iso27001.detail: Runs on ISO/IEC 27001-certified Google Cloud infrastructure.

iso42001.detail: Google Cloud holds ISO 42001 for AI services; Workflows (non-AI) per-service scope is not publicly confirmed.

euAiAct.detail: Serverless workflow orchestration, not an AI system.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary Google Cloud service; no source access.

tools.name: Databricks Lakeflow Jobs
tools.description: Workflow automation for Databricks tasks, pipelines, and ML workloads with DAG, trigger, and observability support.
strengths: Native notebook, pipeline, and ML workflow support
tools.pricing: Databricks workload and job execution costs depend on the underlying compute and services used.
tools.logoNotes: Official Databricks wordmark reused across Databricks product entries because the public Databricks brand portal exposes the vendor lockup, while Lakeflow Jobs does not expose a distinct standalone public mark.
tools.practitionerNote: Strong when orchestration mostly coordinates Databricks notebooks, pipelines, and ML runs, but it is not the same thing as a broad cross-SaaS automation layer.

dataResidency.detail: Workspace data stays in the workspace region; audit logs are region-local.

deployment.detail: Managed SaaS on AWS/Azure/GCP; dedicated single-tenant available; no OSS self-hosted edition.

auditLogging.detail: Audit log system tables capture workspace/account events across the platform.

soc2.detail: Databricks SOC 2 Type II across all clouds/regions.

iso27001.detail: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified across all three clouds.

iso42001.detail: No public ISO 42001 certification listed for Databricks as of 2026-05.

euAiAct.detail: Data pipeline / ML workflow orchestrator, not an AI model provider.

licenseRisk.detail: Proprietary Databricks service.

tools.name: n8n
tools.description: Workflow automation platform with strong self-hosting support and broad integration coverage.
tools.licenseWarning: n8n uses a Sustainable Use License, not MIT or Apache 2.0. It is more accurately described as source-available.
tools.logoNotes: Official n8n wordmark asset from the n8n brand guidelines download page (`logo-dark.svg`).
tools.practitionerNote: Often the fastest path for ops-heavy teams that want broad integrations and self-hosting, though complex enterprise controls may need extra discipline around change management.

dataResidency.detail: n8n Cloud currently EU-only; self-hosted gives customer-controlled residency.

deployment.detail: Self-hosted (primary OSS mode), n8n Cloud (EU SaaS), or hybrid.

auditLogging.detail: Execution insights and log streaming; enterprise Cloud adds access logs; no immutable audit log for self-hosted OSS.

soc2.detail: Annual independent SOC 2 audit; SOC 3 public report; SOC 2 Type II to enterprise customers.

iso27001.detail: ISO 27001 not held; absent from the n8n security page / Trust Center.

iso42001.detail: Not mentioned in n8n public compliance documentation.

euAiAct.detail: Workflow automation tooling, not itself an AI system.

licenseRisk.detail: Sustainable Use License (source-available, fair-code, not OSI); EE features under a separate license.

tools.name: Paperclip
tools.description: Open source control plane for orchestrating persistent AI agent teams, budgets, and approvals.
tools.logoNotes: Official Paperclip favicon/icon asset from the owner-controlled homepage (`/favicon.svg`), verified byte-for-byte against the live source on 2026-05-13 and rendered as a square service icon.
tools.practitionerNote: Interesting control-plane option when budgets, approvals, and persistent agent operations matter, but still earlier than more established workflow stacks.

dataResidency.detail: Fully self-hosted; data stays on customer infrastructure (embedded/external Postgres).

deployment.detail: Self-hosted only (Node.js + Postgres); no vendor-managed cloud.

auditLogging.detail: No dedicated audit-log subsystem documented; OSS codebase is auditable.

soc2.detail: No vendor-managed cloud; SOC 2 falls on the customer's hosting environment.

iso27001.detail: Customer-operated; no managed service to certify.

iso42001.detail: Tooling component; customer-operated.

euAiAct.detail: Open-source agent control-plane component, not an AI model/provider.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: LangFlow
tools.description: Visual flow builder for LLM pipelines and agent workflows with self-hosting support.
tools.statusNote: DataStax Cloud is shutting down April 9, 2026. Self-hosting remains available.
tools.logoNotes: Official Langflow website logo asset (`/images/logo.png`). No separate brand-guidelines or media-kit page was found at review time, so this remains a homepage-sourced project logo pending a stronger primary source.
tools.practitionerNote: Useful for visual experimentation and internal workflow builds, though larger enterprise rollouts should validate long-term governance and platform direction after the IBM/DataStax shift.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted gives customer-controlled residency (DataStax cloud shut down 2026-04).

deployment.detail: Self-hosted (Docker/Kubernetes/local); IBM/DataStax managed cloud deprecated.

auditLogging.detail: No dedicated audit-logging documentation for the self-hosted OSS edition.

soc2.detail: No active managed cloud; self-hosted operators carry their own posture.

iso27001.detail: No active managed cloud to certify.

iso42001.detail: Tooling component; customer-operated.

euAiAct.detail: Visual workflow-builder framework, not an AI model/provider.

licenseRisk.detail: MIT — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Dify
tools.description: Application development and workflow platform for LLM apps with plugin and enterprise features.
strengths: App + workflow surface in one stack
tools.licenseWarning: Dify's modified Apache 2.0 restricts multi-tenant deployment and requires retaining Dify branding without a commercial license.
tools.logoNotes: Official Dify docs/brand-guidelines logo asset (`d05cfc6ebe48f725d171dc71c64a5d16.svg`) from the owner-controlled docs site.
tools.practitionerNote: Good fit for teams that want one self-hostable surface for apps, workflows, and model operations, but the license and commercial boundary need review before standardising widely.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted is customer-controlled; Dify Cloud region not publicly documented.

deployment.detail: Docker/Kubernetes self-hosted, Dify Cloud SaaS, or enterprise on-prem.

auditLogging.detail: No dedicated audit-log documentation confirmed on public Dify docs.

soc2.detail: SOC 2 Type II (Sensiba), second consecutive year.

iso27001.detail: ISO 27001:2022 (Johanson), second consecutive year.

iso42001.detail: Not mentioned in Dify's public compliance documentation.

euAiAct.detail: Application/workflow platform tooling; deployer carries obligations for systems built with it.

licenseRisk.detail: Modified Apache-2.0 — adds multi-tenant and branding restrictions, outside OSI Apache-2.0.

tools.name: Flowise
tools.description: Low-code orchestration and agent workflow builder now under Workday ownership.
tools.logoNotes: Official Flowise docs-site icon asset (`Flowise Logo Dark High Res`) served from the owner-controlled documentation site. GitBook currently delivers the resized file as JPEG.
tools.practitionerNote: Practical low-code orchestration layer for teams moving quickly, provided they validate security posture and ownership-roadmap fit for production use.

dataResidency.detail: Self-hosted is customer-controlled; Flowise Cloud stores data in US East 1 only.

deployment.detail: Self-hosted (Apache-2.0 core) or Flowise Cloud SaaS.

auditLogging.detail: No dedicated audit-log documentation; enterprise tier may add capabilities.

soc2.detail: No SOC 2 certification announced by Flowise directly.

iso27001.detail: No ISO 27001 certification found in Flowise documentation.

iso42001.detail: Not mentioned in Flowise documentation.

euAiAct.detail: Low-code agent/workflow builder tooling, not an AI model/provider.

licenseRisk.detail: Apache-2.0 core + proprietary Commercial License for packages/server/src/enterprise (SSO/RBAC/audit).

tools.name: Haystack
tools.description: LLM pipeline and retrieval orchestration framework rebranded toward the Haystack Enterprise Platform story.
tools.logoNotes: Official Haystack website logo asset (`/images/logos/haystack.png`). No separate brand-guidelines or media-kit page was found at review time, so this remains a homepage-sourced project logo pending a stronger primary source.
tools.practitionerNote: Strong option when retrieval pipelines and modular backend orchestration matter more than no-code UX or broad business-user automation.

dataResidency.detail: Python library runs in-process on customer infrastructure; no vendor data handling.

deployment.detail: OSS library self-hosted (Docker/K8s/VMs); deepset offers a managed Enterprise Platform.

auditLogging.detail: OSS library has no built-in audit log; operators integrate their own.

soc2.detail: OSS library N/A; the deepset-managed Haystack Enterprise Platform holds SOC 2.

iso27001.detail: OSS library N/A; the deepset Enterprise Platform holds ISO 27001.

iso42001.detail: Not mentioned in Haystack/deepset public compliance documentation.

euAiAct.detail: OSS pipeline framework, not an AI model/provider.

licenseRisk.detail: Apache-2.0 — permissive, OSI-approved.

tools.name: Rivet
tools.description: Workflow builder for AI pipelines with desktop-first self-hosting support.
tools.statusNote: No releases in 8+ months. Appears to be in maintenance or slow development.
tools.logoNotes: Official Rivet app logo from the owner-controlled Rivet homepage nav asset (`img/logo.svg`). Kept as a square service icon because the published asset is a square mark rather than a wide wordmark.
tools.practitionerNote: Can still work for narrow visual pipeline use cases, but the maintenance signal makes it a cautious choice for new enterprise standardisation.

dataResidency.detail: Desktop application; data stays on the local machine; no vendor cloud.

deployment.detail: Desktop app (macOS/Windows/Linux) and embeddable Node/TS library; no vendor cloud.

auditLogging.detail: Desktop-only; no server-side audit infrastructure.

soc2.detail: No managed cloud service; user-operated desktop app.
